g.text: optional piece of hardware or software that transforms information provided by a piece of equipment into a form that can be received by an {{term(agent)}}.
software that collects data published from one or more piece(s) of equipment, organizes that data in a structured manner, and responds to requests for data from
client software systems by providing a structured response in the form of a {{term(response document)}} that is constructed using the {{term(semantic data model)}} of a
limit used to trigger warning or alarm indicators.
software or a program that is specific to the solution of an application problem.
{{term(archetype)}} provides the requirements, constraints, and common properties for a type of {{term(Asset)}}.
connection by which one thing is associated with another.
section of an {{term(agent)}} that provides storage for information published from pieces of equipment.
control placed on an element of a {{term(feature)}} such as its size, location, or form, which may be a specification limit, a nominal with tolerance, or some other
{{term(application)}} that sends {{term(request)}} for information to an {{term(agent)}}.
> Note: Examples include software applications or a function that implements the {{term(request)}} portion of an {{term(interface)}} {{term(interaction model)}}.
{{term(standard uncertainty)}} of the result of a measurement when that result is obtained from the values of a number of other quantities, equal to the positive square
root of a sum of terms, the terms being the variances or covariances of these other quantities weighted according to how the measurement result varies with changes in
state transition from `Normal` to either `Warning` or `Fault`.
restricted set of values for a given property.
listing of standardized terms and definitions used in {{term(MTConnect Information Model)}}.
organizes elements of data and standardizes how they relate to one another and to the properties of real-world entities.
{{termplural(key-value pair)}} where each entry is uniquely identified by the {{term(key)}}.
piece of equipment that can produce data that is published to an {{term(agent)}}.
indication that specific content in an {{term(MTConnect Document)}} is currently usable but is regarded as being obsolete or superseded.
indication that specific content in an {{term(MTConnect Document)}} may be changed to {{term(deprecated)}} in a future release of the standard.
piece of written, printed, or electronic matter that provides information or evidence that serves as an official record.
rate of flow of electric charge.
constituent part or a basic unit of identifiable and definable data.
ability for an implementer to extend {{term(MTConnect Information Model)}} by adding content not currently addressed in the MTConnect Standard.
topological entity(ies) or design requirements related to a geometric model. {{cite(QIF 3.0-3.4.59)}}
push or pull on a mass which results in an acceleration.
function that indicates to a {{term(client)}} that the communications connection to an {{term(agent)}} is still viable during times when there is no new data available to
report often referred to as a "keep alive" message.
nested element that is above a lower level element.
specific instantiation of the MTConnect Standard.
rules, relationships, and terminology that are used to define how information is structured.
describes a set of {{term(streaming data)}} in an {{term(agent)}}. Each time an {{term(agent)}} is restarted with an empty {{term(buffer)}}, data placed in the
model that defines how information is exchanged across an {{term(interface)}} to enable interactions between independent systems.
means by which communication is achieved between independent systems.
unique identifier in a {{term(key-value pair)}} association.
association between an identifier referred to as the {{term(key)}} and a value which taken together create a {{term(key-value pair)}}.
place or named space associated with an object or that can be occupied by an object.
first word is lowercase and the remaining words are capitalized and all spaces between words are removed.
nested element that is below a higher level element.
lower conformance boundary for a variable.
lower boundary indicating increased concern and supervision may be required.
identifier representing a consistent set of functionalities defined by the MTConnect Standard.
communication in writing, in speech, or by signals.
data that provides information about other data.
identifier representing a specific set of functionalities defined by the MTConnect Standard.
ideal or desired value for a variable.
act of containing and owning one or more elements.
entity that {{termplural(organize)}} one or more elements.
variable that must be given a value during the execution of a program or a communications command.
discrete item that has both defined and measurable physical characteristics including mass, material, and features, and is created by applying one or more
first letter of each word is capitalized and the remaining letters are in lowercase. All space is removed between letters
supplemental identifier representing only organizational or editorial changes to a {{term(minor)}} version document with no changes in the functionality described in that
method for retaining or restoring information.
{{term(location)}} that is represented by a point in space relative to a reference.
instrument commonly used for measuring the physical geometrical characteristics of an object.
extends a reference metamodel (such as {{term(UML)}}) by allowing to adapt or customize the metamodel with constructs that are specific to a particular domain,
platform, or a software development method.
entity that initiates a {{term(request)}} for information in a communications exchange.
act of reverting back the accumulated value or statistic to their initial value.
> Note: An {{term(Observation)}} with a {{term(data set)}} representation removes all {{termplural(key-value pair)}}, setting the {{term(data set)}} to an empty set.
entity that responds to a {{term(request)}} for information in a communications exchange.
electronic {{term(document)}} published by an {{term(MTConnect Agent)}} in response to a {{term(probe request)}}, {{term(current request)}}, {{term(sample request)}} or
definition of the structure, rules, and vocabularies used to define the information published in an electronic document.
methodology for defining the structure and meaning for data in a specific logical way that can be interpreted by a software system.
mechanism that provides a signal or measured value.
primary key identifier used to manage and locate a specific piece of {{term(streaming data)}} in an {{term(agent)}}.
limit defining a range of values designating acceptable performance for a variable.
mechanism that provides rotational capabilities to a piece of equipment.
> Note: Typically used for either work holding, materials or cutting tools.
{{term(document)}} established by consensus that provides rules, guidelines, or characteristics for activities or their results.. {{cite(ISO/IEC Guide 2:2004)}}
{{term(uncertainty)}} of the result of a measurement expressed as a standard deviation. {{cite(JCGM 100:2008 2.3.1)}}
defines how an existing {{term(UML)}} metaclass may be extended as part of a {{term(profile)}}.
secondary or subordinate type of categorization or classification of information.
two dimensional set of values given by a set of {{termplural(key-value pair)}} {{termplural(table entry)}}.
subdivision of a {{term(table entry)}} representing a singular value.
subdivision of a {{term(table)}} containing a set of {{termplural(key-value pair)}} representing {{termplural(table cell)}}.
element that represents the most significant physical or logical functions of a piece of equipment.
uncertainty (of measurement) parameter, associated with the result of a measurement, that characterizes the dispersion of the values that could reasonably be
> Note: Use of the term uncertainty refers to uncertainty of measurement.
upper conformance boundary for a variable.
upper boundary indicating increased concern and supervision may be required.
unique identifier of the administered item. {{cite(ISO/IEC 11179-:2015)}}
